channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Deputy President Warns Against Ol Kalou Train Amid Electoral Tensions
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has raised concerns about a newly allocated train service in Ol Kalou, warning residents to avoid it due to safety risks. Speaking at his residence, Gachagua a...

item.title: SEC Investigator's Stock Holdings Raise Ethics Concerns Under Trump Administration
item.description: A new report reveals that an unnamed Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) investigator was allowed to retain stock in companies under investigation, raising significant ethical concerns. The waive...

item.title: Veteran Broadcaster and Former Senator Derryn Hinch Passes Away at 82
item.description: Derryn Hinch, a veteran broadcaster and former Australian senator known for his outspoken media career and political activism, has passed away at the age of 82. His long-standing career spanned newspa...

item.title: India's mountain rescue service achieves zero deaths in three years through dedicated efforts
item.description: Hemant Sachdev, a veteran mountaineer, founded the Tiranga Mountain Rescue foundation in 2016 after a life-threatening fall on Mount Everest. His mission was to address the high casualty rates among s...

item.title: Florida Man Sentenced for Ransomware Conspiracy
item.description: Angelo Martino, a Florida man, has been sentenced to over five years in prison for conspiring with hackers to deploy ransomware during his role as a ransomware negotiator for a U.S. cybersecurity comp...

item.title: Cape Town's LGBTQ+ Nightlife Gains Global Recognition with Two Venues Making Time Out's List
item.description: Cape Town's LGBTQ+ nightlife has gained international acclaim as two local venues, Café Manhattan and The Pink Candy Nightclub, were included in Time Out's inaugural global ranking of the world's grea...

item.title: Defense Secretary Teodoro Advocates 4% GDP Defense Budget Amid West Philippine Sea Tensions
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. has called for increasing the Philippines' defense budget to at least 4% of the GDP to address ongoing tensions in the West Philippine Sea (WPS). During an ambus...

item.title: Good Weekend Quiz: Test Your General Knowledge
item.description: The article promotes the Good Weekend quiz, a general knowledge test designed to engage readers. It highlights the quiz's format, encouraging participants to challenge their knowledge. The content als...

item.title: Study finds no increased suicide risk in gender-confused youth after accounting for mental health comorbidities
item.description: The article critiques the misuse of suicide narratives in debates around gender-affirming care, citing a Finnish study of over 2,000 young people that found no evidence of increased suicidality in gen...

item.title: Zanzibar's Political Reconciliation Efforts Amidst Ongoing Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the political landscape of Zanzibar, focusing on the ruling party's dominance and the challenges faced by opposition groups. It highlights the recent developments in political re...

item.title: Microsoft Retires Legacy OWA Light Client in Exchange Server Update
item.description: Microsoft plans to disable and remove OWA Light, a lightweight Outlook Web Access client for Exchange Server, in an upcoming update expected in August 2026. The decision aims to reduce attack surface ...

item.title: Telangana Council Extends LAWCET Counselling Dates
item.description: The Telangana Council of Higher Education (TGCHE) has announced an extension of the LAWCET 2026 counselling period, allowing eligible candidates to complete their online registration and verification ...

item.title: Prince Harry Engages with Invictus Games Participants Amid Personal and Public Challenges
item.description: Prince Harry participated in the Invictus Games event at the National Exhibition Centre in Birmingham, where he engaged with injured military veterans and promoted the cause close to his heart. Despit...

item.title: Marilyn Monroe Recognized as a 1950s Icon in TIME Magazine's '100 Women of the Year' Profile
item.description: This article highlights Marilyn Monroe's selection by TIME Magazine as part of its '100 Women of the Year' project, celebrating her legacy as an iconic figure from the 1950s. While she didn't appear o...

item.title: Nissan Tekton set to enter South African market as local production ends
item.description: Nissan has ended its vehicle production in South Africa after over 60 years, with the new Tekton SUV set to become a key model in the local market. Developed in partnership with Renault in India, the ...

item.title: Beef Price-Fixing Settlement: Companies Agree to $8 Million Payment
item.description: A proposed $8-million settlement has been reached between several major meat producers and Canadian law firms over allegations of price-fixing in the beef industry. Companies including JBS USA, Swift ...

item.title: Spain Prepare for Crucial World Cup Quarterfinal Against Belgium
item.description: Spain's national football team has advanced to the quarterfinals of the FIFA World Cup 2026 without conceding a single goal, setting up a high-stakes encounter against Belgium. Coach Luis de la Fuente...

item.title: Albanese's Comments on Kylie Minogue and Labor's Policy on Gambling and Housing
item.description: The article discusses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's controversial remarks about his attraction to Kylie Minogue during a podcast interview, which sparked significant media backlash. While critics ...

item.title: DNA Testing as Routine Post-Birth Care in Nigeria to Address Paternity Disputes and Hospital Errors
item.description: The article discusses how DNA testing is increasingly becoming essential in Nigeria's post-birth care system. It highlights statistics showing that about 27% of paternity tests conducted by SURJEN Hea...

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth Overrides Suspension of Apache Pilots for Low-Altitude Beach Flights
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has reinstated eight Apache helicopter pilots suspended for flying at low altitudes over crowded South Carolina beaches during the Fourth of July celebrations. T...
